analysis.summary: Overall DQ has slipped from 0.847 to 0.812 (-0.035) over the quarter, driven primarily by Completeness regressions in customers and payments and a small uptick in Timeliness violations on payments.

overall_trend.narrative: Score moved from 0.847 to 0.812, a decline beyond the +/-0.02 stable band.

key_findings: Completeness fell from 0.91 to 0.86 due to higher null rates on customers.phone and customers.credit_score.
Payments table accumulated 38 additional Late/Missed records vs. the prior snapshot.
Validity remained stable at 0.94, so format/range integrity is holding.
risks: If null rates on credit_score continue rising, downstream credit-risk models will degrade.
Late-payment growth signals potential portfolio stress that may breach SLAs next quarter.
recommendations: Patch the upstream loader for customers.phone and customers.credit_score to enforce non-null capture.
Add a Completeness rule with threshold <= 5% on customers.credit_score and alert on breach.
Stand up a weekly trend run instead of quarterly to catch drift earlier.

table_trends.narrative: Null rates climbed on phone and credit_score.
